regions.name: Rome — Ancient Core
regions.description: The Colosseum, Roman Forum, and Palatine Hill — walking through 2,000 years of history

regions.name: Rome — Baroque & Fountains
regions.description: Trevi Fountain, Spanish Steps, Pantheon, and Piazza Navona — the living Baroque city

regions.name: Florence
regions.description: The cradle of the Renaissance — Duomo, Uffizi Gallery, Ponte Vecchio, and rolling Tuscan hills
regions.bestFor: Renaissance art, Tuscan food and wine, day trips to Tuscany

attractions.name: Colosseum
attractions.description: The world's largest ancient amphitheatre, once hosting gladiatorial combat for 80,000 spectators

attractions.name: Roman Forum & Palatine Hill
attractions.description: The heart of ancient Rome — temples, triumphal arches, and the birthplace of the city (combined ticket with Colosseum)

attractions.name: Vatican Museums & Sistine Chapel
attractions.description: One of the world's greatest art collections culminating in Michelangelo's awe-inspiring Sistine Chapel ceiling

attractions.name: Trevi Fountain
attractions.description: Rome's most famous fountain — toss a coin to ensure your return to Rome, best visited at dawn

attractions.name: Pantheon
attractions.description: Perfectly preserved ancient Roman temple converted to a church — the largest unreinforced concrete dome in the world

attractions.name: Piazza Navona
attractions.description: Baroque oval piazza built over Domitian's stadium — three stunning fountains including Bernini's Fountain of the Four Rivers

attractions.name: Florence Duomo (Cathedral & Dome)
attractions.description: Brunelleschi's magnificent terracotta dome dominates the Florence skyline — climb it for extraordinary views

attractions.name: Uffizi Gallery
attractions.description: World's greatest collection of Italian Renaissance paintings — Botticelli, Leonardo, Raphael, Michelangelo

attractions.name: Accademia Gallery (Michelangelo's David)
attractions.description: Home to Michelangelo's 17-foot marble masterpiece David — book weeks in advance

attractions.name: Ponte Vecchio
attractions.description: Florence's iconic medieval bridge lined with jewellery shops, with beautiful views over the Arno at sunset

january.weather: Cold but manageable, 4-12°C Rome, cooler in Florence
pros: Virtually no queues at Colosseum or Uffizi

april.weather: Warm and pleasant, 12-20°C, some spring showers
notableEvents: Easter (Pasqua) — Rome is spectacular at Easter
packingTips: Comfortable walking shoes — cobblestones are hard on feet
cons: Book Vatican and Uffizi months ahead

october.weather: Mild and comfortable, 14-22°C, ideal walking weather
pros: Grape harvest in Tuscany for day trips
cons: Getting busier as autumn high season approaches

culturalTips: Dress modestly to enter churches — cover shoulders and knees; carry a scarf or shawl
Book the Colosseum, Vatican, and Uffizi online weeks in advance — queues without booking are hours long
The €1-2 standing espresso at a bar counter is the Italian way — sit-down service costs double
Restaurants add a 'coperto' (cover charge) of €1-3 per person — this is normal and legal
Pasta is not a side dish in Italy — it is a first course (primo). Order pasta, then meat or fish
Tap water is safe and free — ask for 'acqua del rubinetto' to avoid expensive bottled water
Pickpocketing is common near major tourist sites — use a money belt and be vigilant
Gelato from a gelateria with covered tubs (not piled high in bright colors) is the genuine article

budget.description: Charming neighborhoods with great local food and guesthouses at $70-110/night

mid.description: Central, walkable, close to main sites, 3-star hotels at $140-250/night

luxury.description: Boutique luxury hotels, rooftop terraces with views, $300-700/night
